summary:
Claim: A minimum daily dose of 20 mg/kg is required for tetracycline to cause tooth discoloration.  
Evidence: - Clinical studies confirm visible dental staining begins at or above 20 mg/kg/day in developing teeth. - Pediatric guidelines recommend avoiding tetracyclines in children under 8 years due to risk of permanent discoloration starting at this dosing level.  
Answer: B
